content.problemStatement:
That covers the fundamentals of the STL. Delving into the entire C++ STL isn't essential since not all components are commonly used.

Before concluding this module, it's worth noting a few other valuable containers that are widely utilized. However, we won't delve into them here since they are already discussed in other courses:

- linked list
- stack/queue
- priority_queue
content.inputFormat:
- First line will contain $T$, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow. 
- The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $Z$.
- The second line contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1,A_2,…,A_N$ the attacking power of the soldiers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line. If Pain cannot be defeated,  this line should contain the string "Evacuate" (without quotes). Otherwise, it should contain the minimum number of times the soldiers need to attack.
